It started to rain heavily. Just then, the hen-pigeon saw a bird catcher coming near the tree. In the cage, he had the cock-pigeon. It was unconscious.

Fear gripped the hen-pigeon. The bird catcher neared the tree. She had to hide, but she also wanted a closer look to see if it was her friend. She flew down to the lowest branch.  It was her friend ! The hen-pigeon was worried.
Soon it stopped raining, the bird catcher wanted to leave, but it was too dark and late in the night. He thought to camp there for the night and leave in the morning.

The hen-pigeon began to cry, and her friend comforted her, advising her not to feel sad. Her friend mentioned that they had a guest who was shivering and hungry, indicating that the man required her help.

The pigeon flew from the tree in search of dry twigs. She got two or three twigs at a time and made a big heap of dry twigs. She got flintstones for the bird catcher to start the fire. The bird catcher was surprised by the hen-pigeon. He made fire.

Once a beggar was hungry. He saw a big house and went there to see if he would get something to eat. The house was very big and luxurious. There was a beautiful garden around the house. As he neared the house, he could smell tasty food. He knocked at the door.
The rich man shouted angrily at the beggar and  asked him to leave. The old man again requested him for some food. The rich man asked him to get out of his sight, and slammed the door.
The old man slowly walked away. On his way, he saw a lady from the house dropping banana leaves with food in the trash can.
The old man slowly walked to another house and knocked on the door, inquiring if anyone was home. A voice responded, claiming nobody was asked who was speaking if nobody was home.

The man got irritated and opened the door and asked him to try the big house on the same street. The old man sadly replied, that he had tried, but did not get any food. The man then asked him how did he expect help from others when the richest man in the village himself had denied all help. The old man then left the place.

The old man was tired and could not walk anymore. So he sat under a tree and soon slept. A man in shabby clothes woke him and inquired who he was and why he was lying there. The old man replied, that he did not have any food or a place to stay.

The poor man told the old man that he looked tired and hungry and invited him to come to his home and stay with him. The old man replied that he didn’t want to be a burden to him and only needed some food. The poor man replied that , that was okay and that he could just come to have some food with them. He then took the old man to his hut.

The hut was very small and barely enough for the family. The old man saw that the house wouldn’t withstand the monsoon. The house had no furniture, but only a few utensils were there for use. The people in the house, a lady and two children, were very happy to welcome the old man in. They made him sit comfortably. The children sat near him.

The man introduced himself as Kaliyan. He said that he worked on the farm nearby. He also introduced his wife Viji and his children Gopi and Rathika. Soon, the children got close to the old man. They asked many questions and played with him. Kaliyan helped the man wash his face and hands.

When the old man entered the hut, Viji was ready to serve the food. They all sat in a circle. Viji served the gruel from an earthen pot. First, she gave to the old man, and then to the children and Kaliyan. After dinner, Kaliyan laid a mat for the old man to sleep on.

The next morning, the beggar asked them to pack their things. They all couldn’t understand why he asked so. Before they could ask anything, the beggar revealed that he was a  land lord from a nearby town. His family and him were helping the poor and the needy.

Gopal shot himself as Chinese were going to capture him. A grenade blast killed Sela. Nura, the tribal girl, was captured alive.

From then on, he was able to keep a calendar. On the island, he found many kinds of goats and hares, which he shot and ate.

Then, he made a small boat.  One day, Raja found a footprint of a human, on the other side of the island. He was frightened. For two years, he lived in fear. Then, one day he saw some tribal men chasing a young fellow. Raja took out his gun and shot twice.  The tribal men got scared and ran away.

He named him Friday as he met him on Friday. At last, he had a human friend in the island. Raja taught Friday to speak in English, to shoot and eat like a human  One day, they both saved some people from the cannibals. Among them, they found Friday’s father and his friends.  They stayed with Raja and Friday for a few days and left the island.

Another day, Friday informed Raja about a ship. They both hid and watched the ship. Raja learnt that the ship’s men had rebelled against the captain. After killing some of the rebels, Raja, Friday and the captain retook the ship. At last, Raja sailed from the island to England with Friday, after twenty-eight years, two months and nineteen days.

The window curtains flutter and gently brush my face. Who makes it happen? Vayu, the wind!
A bolt of lightning far away. Black clouds moving my way. Who makes that happen? Vayu, the wind!
A glass tumbler on a window sill, crashes on the ground. Thank God, I was not around. Who played this mischief? Of course, it was Vayu, the wind!
Cannot be seen. Cannot be heard. Does all the work without a word Who can it be? Of course! The wind!
Branches sway and leaves tremble. Flowers gently fall. Who did it all? Vayu, the wind!
The milk in my cup- too hot, too hot. But soon it is ready for me to gulp. Who makes that happen? Vayu, the wind!
Far from the house, we are playing, yet, I can smell the sweets mother is preparing. Who makes it happen? Vayu, the wind!
A whistle blows. A train rolls in. I cannot see, but hear its din. Who makes that happen? Vayu, the wind!
Everytime I finish my hot, hot bath. My wet body feels so cool, cool cool. Who makes that happen? Vayu, the wind!
Not so long ago, there was a King. The King had a pet elephant.
A roly-poly elephant. A chubby-happy elephant.
One day the King said out aloud, I wonder how much my elephant weighs?
So the King called Lilavati. Lilavati thought. And thought. And thought. At last she had an idea!
And got the chubby-happy little elephant into a boat.
Then she got the elephant out of the boat. And the boat was filled up with stones. When the boat sank up to the mark, Lilavati said, stop!
The King declared, find out the weight of my elephant.
Even the court scientists tried.At last one of them said, My little daughter Lilavati can help us.
She led the King’s roly-poly elephant to the river.
The boat sank and sank and sank. When it sank no more, Lilavati made a mark on the boat.
Now weigh the stones. And subtract my weight.
And that, O King, is the weight of your elephant!

text: Gopal : I am hungry. Prepare some salad for me.
Kavi played a song that her friend had taught her. Meena started clicking pictures of the event with his camera.

Grandpa smiled and called Kavi. He gave her the gift. Her savings were used to buy the gift. Kavi eagerly opened the box and found a brand-new flute. Her eyes were moist with tears. The whole family asked her to play the flute.
Kavi followed the dry stream bed down the hill. At the bottom was another tank. It had lots of goats but no water.
I am sure we will race with our cycles and play kabaddi. What are you going to do?
Meena walked into the kitchen where Kavi was cleaning the vessels. Near the kitchen sink Meena saw an old cracked cup with coffee in it. After finishing all the work at home Kavi drank the coffee.

They bought every single type of food available across the shops. On the second day Jayan got sick due to food poisoning. So he decided to spend the money only on toys. The eldest of the kids were Kavi and Meena. They were determined to save the money and use it purposefully. So they controlled their desires and saved every rupee they got.
The next day Kavi brushed her teeth with muddy tank water in a tiny glass.
Kavi and Meena began climbing up the hill. The stream here was dry as well.
Kavi had an idea. She hugged the pump. You can’t turn it on now! Meena ran up to hug the pump too.
Mom: Just a minute. Kavi it’s for you.
Kavi and Meena looked up at the sky. The sun glared back at them. Everything was white-hot and dusty.
Kavi and Meena decided to draw a map of their village and all its tanks and streams showing all the places where the water might have flowed.
The monsoon is here! Kavi shouted.
The water has been found! Mystery solved! Kavi said.
As Gopal and Kavi felt hungry they returned to parents. But John sped towards the waterfall. He was shocked to see that no one was there.
On a nice winter morning John's family set out on a trip to the nearby hill. John's cousins Raja Gopal Anu and Kavi accompanied him. The kids were so excited to explore the place.
Kavi was suddenly angry. She stomped her foot.
Kavi: Yes! It was real fun Anu. We were happy.
Have it, Kavi.  This is not much.
Kavi:Look at that naughty monkey Let us act like it and have fun
Anu and Kavi are very good friends.

It was a sunny afternoon after a long time in Ooty. Meena and Gopal were sitting on their balcony. The last three vacations were very exciting for them as they were involved in solving three mysterious cases in their city. They missed their friends John Anu and Kavi.

Everyone in the family shared about the year gone one by one. After dinner Meena brought his new camera and told grandpa how he had saved and bought the camera. Grandpa was very proud and happy. He congratulated him.  Meanwhile Kavi‛s father gave grandpa a gift and said something in his ears.

At that time her father needed money urgently and asked if he could use her savings. He promised to return the money soon. Kavi gave her savings to her father. Although she was proud of helping her father she still wanted to buy the flute. She was disappointed.  Soon it was time to visit the village. Everyone was happy to meet each other after one year. On the day of the car festival the whole family gathered in the village.
The tank in Kavi’s village was almost dry. Mother began to pray for rain. Mother collected all the buckets and pots and vessels in the house and filled them up.
Kavi asked the goatherd Do you know where the water might be?
Where are you taking our water? Kavi wanted to know.
Can I help? said a voice. It was Meena who had seen Kavi running away from school.
Gopal’s parents left the village Kavi said. They went to the city where they have water. Maybe we should all go.

Meenas dream was to buy a camera. At the end of the year his father checked his savings and bought him the latest camera with his savings!  Kavi had a clear plan. She had a friend named Anandhi. Anandhi‛s father was a flutist. They lived in the same street so the girls used to visit each other often. On many of these occasions Anandhi used to teach her to play the flute. Kavi always wanted to buy a flute. At the end of one year she managed to save some amount.
Kavi loved solving mysteries: Like the time Mother couldn’t find her reading glasses.
Kavi had found them in her book marking the page she was reading. I’ll find that water Kavi muttered to herself.
My friend is a Sanitary Engineer Kavi yelled. She knows what’s fair.
Fine Kavi said. And she stomped back home. But it wasn’t fine! There was still no water still no rain.
Finally they sat back and pored over the map. We haven’t seen that tank yet Kavi pointed to one of the tanks they had drawn.
Kavi collapsed into the boat with Meena and gnawed on a lotus stem.
Kavi : She taught poem and gave homework.

Kavi and Anu were watching all this from afar. They got worried and rushed with Raja towards the Inspector. Raja started barking and jumping on the Inspector. The inspector was annoyed with the dog. John saw it as a chance to run from there. He hid in a bush nearby. Inspector joe ran towards the old man and caught him.

This news alarmed the group. Kavi told everyone to rush to the city as some message will be passed regarding this. Within no time the 5D was in front of the old mans bench. Everyone sat at different places to keep an eye on the man. After an hour a man came and sat next to the old man. The old man was busy drawing something on the ground with his stick.
Kavi: Of course. I need to renew my membership.
Anu: Hi Kavi, Am I audible? This is Anu. How are you?
Gopal : Thanks Kavi, what did ma’am teach in tamil?
John Anu Kavi and Raja came to Meena and Gopals house. Raja was jumping with joy and continuously wagging his tail.
Kavi : Don’t worry, I will explain it.
Kavi: Ok, I will be ready.
Anu: Hello! May I please speak to Kavi?
Kavi: I‘m not able to hear you. Speak a little louder.

Meena narrated the entire dream to her parents. Meena parents didn’t say a word and remained very quiet. The same day at 9 am Teddy ran to the gate with his pillow. It was Kavi again. Kavi started doing her routine of cleaning dishes. Meena walked into the kitchen to see a new coffee cup on the counter. Meena smiled and walked back to the room with a renewed hope.
Kavi: My name is Kavi. I am lost.
Kavi: Thank you. But my parents and teachers advised me not to go with any stranger.
Kavi: I was in a party.
Kavi: What a surprise! I am good. What is the matter?

This time it was Kavi. She was a differently abled person who sells candles outside a church. Teddy ran to her and would not stop wagging his tail until she pet him. They continued walking. Teddy and Meena finally reached home and were ready to welcome guests. Meena’s family had a great time with Anu aunty and her son. After a long day they all went to sleep.

Meena was sleeping and suddenly saw flashes of the broken coffee cup leftover food the street dog getting hit by stones in her dream. Meena woke up very disturbed. She looked around. She drank water and went back to sleep. After a while Meena saw flashes of Teddy playing with Kavi John and Raja. Meena woke up again but this time with tears filled in her eyes.
Kavi: I came here with my parents for the car festival.
Kavi: Sorry. I am looking for a policeman or traffic police for my guidance.
Anu: Where were you last night Kavi?
Kavi: They were also with me.
Kavi: If so I will give my father’s phone number. Please call him. But I will not come with you.
Chittu asked about Kavin and Geetha
That’s where I came from Kavi said. No water there.
Without noticing the other three kept running towards the waterfall. There Gopal and Kavi got diverted on seeing a naughty monkey.
Kavi: Hey! He was with me. She was at home.
Anu: What about Kavin and Geetha?
When they reached the tank Kavi and Meena realised they were wrong. This tank was full!
Kavi walked to the other side of the tank past dead fish and dried reeds. Do you know where the water could have gone? Kavi asked a fisherman. Downstream? the fisherman suggested.

Kavi climbed up to the tank. Then she climbed up some more to a tank further up the hill. There was no water no birds. There was only one person there. Meena I can’t find any water. Any idea where it’s gone? Kavi called to her. She knew Meena from school.

Teddy Kavi akka left an hour ago and where are you running again? asked Meena. Meena again ran after him. This time it was John. John collects garbage from every house. Teddy would not stop wagging his tail until John pet him. Meena ran to the kitchen to pick up the garbage bag.

Kavi got out her notebook and pencil. She put on her thinking cap and followed her parents to the tank. She examined the tank bed closely. It was cracked and dusty. Kavi wondered Where did the water in the tank go? Did it run away? Was it stolen? This is a mystery!

Teddy parents are here and where are you running again? asked Meena. Meena again ran after him. This time it was Kavi the house helper at the gate. Teddy would not stop wagging his tail and followed her until she pet him. Kavi then started cleaning the house and every time she walked near Teddy he would wag his tail.

text: Cricket is played with a bat and ball, but hockey is played with a stick and ball.

mech1_question: {"content_body": "{\"level\": \"S1\", \"mechanics\": \"askMore\", \"data\": {\"images\": {\"imageOne\": \"10758df5-f2c6-47a8-84e6-3fccc286a634.png\", \"imageTwo\": \"29a6bb4e-7a2b-42c4-8652-ff24c488344a.png\"}, \"conversation\": [{\"speaker\": \"What did you feel when you saw Earth from space?\", \"user\": \"My heart floated like a balloon filled with wonder.\", \"audio\": \"41897884-e79b-4f80-bd07-2b9436a0ff4f.mp3\", \"speakerAudio\": \"f5058193-6b29-43b8-a496-6995efcaee0b.mp3\"}, {\"speaker\": \"Were you scared during takeoff into the sky?\", \"user\": \"My stomach flipped like a boat on wild waves.\", \"audio\": \"1b33b69c-b1e8-48ff-91cb-526f3255a7cc.mp3\", \"speakerAudio\": \"bfaa95b8-548b-48d3-b0c3-21a653eeb5fc.mp3\"}, {\"speaker\": \"Did you ever feel lonely in the spaceship?\", \"user\": \"Yes, silence sat beside me like a quiet friend.\", \"audio\": \"a53fe531-9a5d-471b-a761-88f1c752f25f.mp3\", \"speakerAudio\": \"747e2d21-cddb-4e8a-80f1-6d1544bd353b.mp3\"}, {\"speaker\": \"What was your favourite moment in the whole journey?\", \"user\": \"I touched nothing, but felt everything in that silence.\", \"audio\": \"5e0597fc-2a68-4b2f-8e5e-5b233a829dbc.mp3\", \"speakerAudio\": \"608fb238-e733-4cc2-92f2-7bfaef680354.mp3\"}, {\"speaker\": \"What do stars look like from up so high?\", \"user\": \"They shine brighter, like diamonds on a black blanket.\", \"audio\": \"e6f56b1f-f978-4778-b9f9-d9d7470ea494.mp3\", \"speakerAudio\": \"5afa50f5-3529-4e46-83f3-6af829a6de15.mp3\"}]}}", "language": "en", "mechanics_id": "Mechanic_8"}
